title: herniaria erckertii subsp. pulvinata
intro: Herniaria erckertii subsp. pulvinata, also known as alpine rampion or rampion, is a flowering plant species of the family Caryophyllaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Herniaria erckertii subsp. pulvinata are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
